source_pr.title: statistics: refactor the statistics package use the RestrictedSQLExecutor API
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: 
For https://github.com/pingcap/tidb-test/issues/1152

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?


What's Changed:

How it Works:

Code refactor using the secure API.

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- No code


### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note

symptoms: Go race detector reports a data race between stats workers sharing a session (StmtCtx reset vs warning append) during auto analyze
failure_signature: DATA RACE in statistics handle workers (ResetContextOfStmt vs StmtCtx warnings append)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to a data race in the statistics handle: `updateStatsWorker()` and `autoAnalyzeWorker()` could share the same session, where one goroutine resets statement context while another appends warnings to `StmtCtx`, triggering race-detector failures (see issue #21393). This PR stabilizes it by executing auto analyze with the "ignore warning" option so warnings are not appended to the shared `StmtCtx`, avoiding the concurrent mutation that causes the race.
fix_pattern: Avoid concurrent mutation of a shared session `StmtCtx` between stats workers (e.g., run auto analyze with ignore-warnings / isolate session state) to prevent ResetContext-vs-warning-append data races.

evidence: evidence: // Ignore warnings to get rid of a data race here https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/21393

files: domain/domain.go, server/statistics_handler.go, session/session.go, statistics/handle/bootstrap.go, statistics/handle/ddl.go, statistics/handle/dump.go, statistics/handle/dump_test.go, statistics/handle/gc.go, statistics/handle/handle.go, statistics/handle/handle_test.go, statistics/handle/update.go, types/datum.go
